chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now, let us look at analysis of quick sort.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Let us analyze it.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Find out its time complexity.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: See one important thing is it's a recursive and it is following divide and conquer strategy.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: It is using partitioning procedure, partitioning algorithm and the partitioning algorithm will

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: find out the position of pivot and it will divide the list.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Suppose I have the list of elements, let us say 1 through 15.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: I have a list of 15 elements.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Then if I call quick sort by passing first index as 1 and last index as 15.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Suppose it is partitioning at the middle.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Then I am getting elements from 1 to 7 on this side and 9 to 15 on this side.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So partitioning was done at index 8 that is at the middle of a list.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So elements 1 to 7 here and 9 to 15 on this side.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Suppose the partitioning is done in the middle.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Assume this.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Then what about this left hand side, this part and this part.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Assume always partitioning is in the middle.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Then here the middle element will be 4 and this side we get a list from 1 to 3 and in

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: this side 5 to 7.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Similarly if it is happening here then the partitioning will be at 12 middle of a list

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: then we get the elements 9 to 11 here and 13 to 15.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Suppose partitioning is again in the middle then I get one element on this side that is

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: 1 to 1 and 3 to 3.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Here the partitioning is on 4 so then 5 to 5 and 7 to 7.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Partitioning is at 10 here 9 to 9 here 11 to 11 and here 13 to 13, 14 partitioning is

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: done 15 to 15.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: If partitioning is always done in the middle I have given 15 elements and suppose the partitioning

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: was at 8 and that's all for every node I have written partitioning.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So the working of the quick sort will be like this.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Then if I analyze this one based on the tree what the partitioning algorithm is doing.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: It is comparing the element and exchanging them.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So what is the time taken by this algorithm?

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: It is n.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: It will go through entire list.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So the time taken for this is n and the time taken at this level this left side and right

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: side together is almost n.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: No doubt one element is reduced but let us say it is n.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Then at this level also it is n and that's all that is the end of the level.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Then what is the height of a tree?

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: See it was 15 divided by 2 as the partitioning is always in the middle half half this side

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: divided by 2 by 2 by 2 when something is getting divided by 2 every time.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: How many times it will divide such that it reaches 1.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So if you say n by 2 then by 2 then by 2.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So how many times such that it becomes 1, 1, 1 element.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this n by 2 power k is equals to 1 and n is equals to 2 power k.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So k is log n base 2.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So yes how many levels are there?

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Log n levels are there and in each level what is the time taken I am assuming that partitioning

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: algorithm will compare the elements for n times.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So it is n.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So the time complexity of quick sort will be order of n log n.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Assume that always partitioning is done in the middle of a list.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So that assumption has given us this time.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this will be best case of an algorithm.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: What is the case if the partitioning is always in the middle and what is the time in best

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: case it's order of n log n.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So the best case time of quick sort is n log n and this we are going to achieve only

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: if the partitioning is done in the middle.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Let us see whether this best case is possible or not.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: For that let us know about median.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: What is median?

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: If I have the numbers 1 2 3 4 5 6 7 the numbers are sorted and this is the middle element

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: of the sorted list and this is called as median.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So median is the middle element of sorted list.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Then best case of quick sort is always the partitioning should be in the middle.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So if partitioning is in the middle means the element that is selected as pivot should

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: be a median.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So how can you know median unless the list is sorted.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: We cannot know the median.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So is it possible that always the element selected is a median and it is sitting in

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: the middle of a list partition in the middle of a list.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: It's not possible.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Randomly it may happen but we cannot control it.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So achieving best case is not possible in quick sort.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: You cannot achieve it may randomly happen that always the element is median.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: What is the worst case of quick sort?

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Suppose I have a list 2 4 8 10 16 18 17.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Suppose I have a list which is already sorted.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Then if I perform quick sort how it works?

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Partition algorithm we saw it will select the first element as pivot and it will start

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: i here and j here.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: i will continue until it finds a greater number so here and j will continue until it finds

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: a smaller or equal number so it's not small not small it's not so it will come and stop

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: here.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Then where the partitioning will be done at the same place.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now this is sorted so rest of the elements we have to sort.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So you can see that partitioning will be done in the beginning of a list because the list

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: was already sorted.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now from this list if I select 4 where it will be partitioned where it will be positioned

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: finally at the same place again.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So always the partitioning will be happening in the beginning of a list.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: That is the worst case of quick sort.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: If I give the numbers from 1 to 7 then 1 will be sorted and on this side I get 2 to

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: 7 then 2 will be sorted and this side I will get the number from 3 to 7 then 4 to 7 then

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: 5 to 7 6 to 7 and 7 to 7.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now you can see that always the partitioning is happening on the beginning of a list or

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: it may happen at the end of the list also it depends here it is all sorted in ascending

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: order if it is in descending order always will be at the end.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now what is the time taken here first partitioning algorithm takes makes n comparisons then n-1

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: comparisons n-2 and so on so 2 then 1.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So what is this equal to?

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This is equal to n into n plus 1 by 2 that is order of n square.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: That's it.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: The worst case time of quick sort is n square.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Worst case time is order of n square.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Best case time of quick sort is n log n which is not possible for us to achieve it but worst

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: case time is n square and when it will happen if the list is already sorted.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this is the problem with quick sort that its worst case is n square.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: If you compare this with the merge sort merge sort was not having any best or worst cases

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: it was just average time n log n but here the average time is n log n but the worst

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: case is n square.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now we need to solve this one.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: How can we reduce this problem?

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: See the probability that if the list is already sorted we are again sorting it the chances

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: are high because we want to keep the list in the sorted order we may be sorting it again

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: and again.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So if it is already sorted and you are sorting it then the time is going to be n square.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So this is the problem with quick sort.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So what we do is we say that don't always select the first element as pivot.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: You can select the middle element as pivot.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: If you select middle element then what happens?

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: If you select this one where it will come?

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: It will come here only so the partitioning will be done here.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: If partitioning is happening here then the list gets divided into two equal halves.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: That's it.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: If always we select the middle element as pivot then the partitioning will be done always

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: in the middle of a list because the list is already sorted and that's how it will convert

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: from n square to n log n.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: The worst case will become best case.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: There are two suggestions for improving the worst case of quick sort.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Removing worst case of quick sort.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Always select middle element as pivot.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So if the list is already sorted it will become best case that is n log n.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: But still the maximum time will be n square for some element some arrangement of elements.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Other suggestion is randomly select some element as pivot.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: If you randomly select some element as pivot again it may be possible that it may take

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: n log n time or maximum again n square time.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So always the worst time taken by quick sort is n square.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: About the space complexity quick sort is a recursive algorithm and it doesn't need any

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: extra space but it will use a stack.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So what could be the maximum size of the stack?

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: In worst case it may be n.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: What is the minimum size of the stack?

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: In best case the height may be log n.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So the quick sort takes stock size that is log n to n.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So how you can know this?

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Just now I have shown trees in best case the height of a tree was log n.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So the size of the stack depends on the height of a tree tracing tree and in worst case it

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: was n.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So maximum size of the stack may be n.

chunks.title: QuickSort Analysis - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So that's all about the quick sort.
